commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Needs maintainer/security review and likely Prism-side API work before this dependency swap can safely land.; Needs attention: the PR moves untrusted image temp files to Prism's generic temp workspace and introduces a commit-pinned GitHub tarball dependency with build approval.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve the OpenClaw temp workspace for native tools — src/media/image-ops.ts:71-78 (sha=552ddd9315d89d0967a143682169a6a6ee6a51e1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces OpenClaw's inline Photon/media-understanding image operations with a small `@openclaw/prism` adapter, updates dependency pin/shrinkwrap handling, and adjusts image/media tests.

PR surface: Source -1882, Tests -101, Config -2, Other +22. Total -1963 across 19 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding from source inspection: current `main` uses `resolvePreferredOpenClawTmpDir()` and has a temp-dir test, while the pinned Prism source uses `mkdtemp(os.tmpdir())`. I did not execute the native fallback path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Needs maintainer/security review and likely Prism-side API work before this dependency swap can safely land.

target.title: refactor: use Prism for image processing
target.body:
## Summary

- Move OpenClaw image processing to `@openclaw/prism`, pinned to `openclaw/prism@a2b030f46524c037be247d213d533fd212d9eced`.
- Delete the bundled media-understanding Photon image shim and keep `src/media/image-ops.ts` as a small Prism adapter.
- Preserve trusted native tool resolution, `windows-native` aliases, best-effort EXIF normalization, input/output pixel limits, and shrinkwrap/pin guard compatibility for commit-pinned GitHub tarballs.

## Verification

- Prism: `pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm build && pnpm pack --pack-destination /tmp/prism-pack`
- Prism CI: https://github.com/openclaw/prism/actions/runs/26412129728
- OpenClaw focused tests: `pnpm test src/media/image-ops.prism-adapter.test.ts src/media/image-ops.input-guard.test.ts src/media/image-ops.helpers.test.ts src/media/web-media.test.ts src/agents/tool-images.test.ts src/gateway/managed-image-attachments.test.ts test/package-manager-config.test.ts test/scripts/check-dependency-pins.test.ts test/scripts/generate-npm-shrinkwrap.test.ts`
- OpenClaw changed gate: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`
- OpenClaw build: `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm build`
- Auto review: `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw no longer owns its image-processing Photon implementation directly; core media image operations delegate to Prism while preserving safety and backend behavior.
Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node/pnpm repo defaults, real `@openclaw/prism` dependency fetched from the pushed GitHub commit.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`; `pnpm test ...`; `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm check:changed`; `env -u OPENCLAW_TESTBOX pnpm build`; Prism CI on `a2b030f46524c037be247d213d533fd212d9eced`.
Evidence after fix: focused OpenClaw tests passed 5 Vitest shards; `check:changed` passed dependency pins, shrinkwrap, typecheck, lint, and import cycles; build completed; Prism CI run 26412129728 passed.
Observed result after fix: image metadata/resizing/PNG optimization/agent attachment paths remain green, native fallback commands still route through OpenClaw trusted resolution, and shrinkwraps stay aligned with the pinned Prism package.
What was not tested: Windows `windows-native` PowerShell execution was preserved in Prism and typechecked, but not live-run on a Windows host in this local pass.
target.author: steipete

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
